pages.raw_text:
Hundreds gathered last night at
Elks temple to hear the speeches
against the proceedings of the trial
of Meyer, Pettibone and Haywood,
the officers of the Western Fedora
ton of Miners, on a charge of in
stigating the murder of Ex-Governor
Steunenberg of Idaho. Yesterday
was the first anniversary of the
arrest. Resolutions were adopted
condemning the legal proceedings
as similar to Russian despotism.


The principal speaker was ex-
Governor ID. c. Coates, of Colorado.
He predicted that Meyer, Pettibonc
and Haywood would walk out of
Jail exonerated He rehearsed the
history of their arrest from the be-
ginning, telling how Pinkerton de-
tectives kidnapped them in Colorado
and sent them to Idaho. He scored
the supreme court and attorneys in
terested in the case.


Much applause greeted James r.
Sovereign, who has addressed labor
bodies in this city for many years.
Other speakers were H. l.. Hughes,
who presided at the meeting, and
A. w. Jones, of the cigarmakers
union.
